decisions.reasoning: Flare Blitz is a strong 2HKO move that punishes Tornadus-Therian for staying in. Since I am not in immediate danger of being KO'd, applying offensive pressure is the best way to force a switch or weaken the opponent.
Flare Blitz is a 2HKO on Tornadus-Therian, and since I am ahead in the exchange, I should press the attack to secure the KO before Tornadus can pivot out or heal with Regenerator.

Incineroar is at 35% HP and will be OHKOed by Urshifu's Close Combat or 2HKOed by Surging Strikes. Switching to Amoonguss provides a crucial resistance to Water-type moves and allows me to threaten Urshifu with Giga Drain or Spore.
Urshifu-Rapid Strike is forced to commit to a move, and Spore is the highest value play to neutralize the threat or force a switch, as Amoonguss survives the incoming Close Combat.

The opponent's Urshifu is already asleep based on the HP status provided. Spore will fail, but since Amoonguss is at 41% and Urshifu is the immediate threat, using Spore or Protect is standard; however, since the opponent is likely to switch out their sleeping Urshifu, Spore is the safest play to catch a potential switch-in or force them to stay in and waste a turn.

The opponent's Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep, and since Amoonguss is slow, using Spore again ensures that if they wake up, they are immediately put back to sleep, neutralizing their offensive pressure.
The opponent's Urshifu is currently asleep, making Spore the optimal play to maintain control or force a switch while keeping Amoonguss on the field to absorb potential hits.
The opponent's Urshifu-Rapid is already asleep, so I will re-apply Spore to ensure it stays incapacitated while I maintain board control.

Urshifu-Rapid-Strike is already asleep, and since Amoonguss is at low HP, attempting to keep the opponent asleep or forcing a switch is the best way to maintain momentum without sacrificing a healthy sweeper.
The opponent's Flutter Mane is currently asleep. Using Spore again ensures it stays incapacitated, preventing it from attacking or setting up while I maintain board control.
Flutter Mane is already asleep, so refreshing Spore is the optimal play to keep it neutralized while Amoonguss is on the field, preventing it from attacking upon waking up.
Amoonguss has fainted; I must send out a new Pokemon. Incineroar is the best pivot to check Flutter Mane's special attacks and potentially use Intimidate or Knock Off to disrupt the opponent.
Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on Flutter Mane. Even if the opponent switches, securing the KO on the active threat is the highest priority to prevent further damage to my team.
Incineroar is at 13% and will be OHKOed by Urshifu's Close Combat. Switching to Calyrex-Ice preserves Incineroar for a potential Intimidate pivot later and brings in a healthier threat.
Urshifu-Rapid is currently asleep, making this the optimal turn to deal significant damage. High Horsepower provides better damage output than Glacial Lance against the Fighting/Water typing.

Calyrex-Ice is slower and will be 3HKO'd by Urshifu-Rapid, while it cannot KO Urshifu in return. Switching to Kingambit provides a defensive pivot that resists Urshifu's likely moves and can threaten back with Sucker Punch or Kowtow Cleave.
Incineroar is fainted; I must send out Calyrex-Ice to handle the Urshifu-Rapid-Strike, as it is the only remaining option that can threaten it effectively.

Calyrex-Ice is slower and will be 3HKO'd by Close Combat while only 3HKOing Urshifu back. Switching to Dragonite allows me to tank the hit and threaten a KO or force a switch, preserving Calyrex-Ice for later.
